spec_match.rationale: Floater uses anchor-size() in `margin-left`, `min-width`, AND `max-width` (per the visible code) — uses the new ability in margins as well as keeping the sizing usage. Hits the spec change cleanly.

interactive.rationale: Slider drives card scale; live readout shows actual measured card width AND computed margin-left in pixels, so the visitor can verify the 0.3x multiplier really applies.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises a proportional design-system spacing pattern, the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: The displayed code includes a `max-width: calc(anchor-size(--card width) * 0.8)` line that the actual stylesheet does NOT apply — the code sample lies about what the rendered demo uses. Also no fallback for browsers without inset/margin anchor-size() support.

references.rationale: See also has no chromestatus.com link and no spec link — sibling concept links only. Breaks CLAUDE.md rule #3.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout; rose-on-ivory and emerald-on-ivory both pass WCAG AA; no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com link to see-also
openQuestions.detail: Rule #3: this page must embed https://chromestatus.com/feature/5203950077476864. Currently only sibling links present.

openQuestions.title: Reconcile the displayed code with the applied CSS
openQuestions.detail: The 'the api' section shows a `max-width: calc(anchor-size(--card width) * 0.8)` line but the live stylesheet does not include it. Either add the property to .floater so the code sample is honest, or drop it from the sample.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate min-width clamping at very small scales
openQuestions.detail: When the user drags the slider to 0.7x, the floater's min-width should keep it readable. There's no readout showing min-width vs natural width — add a third row to the readout grid so visitors see the clamp behaviour land.

summary: Solid, well-instrumented demo with a live readout that verifies the maths. Missing chromestatus reference and the displayed code sample includes a property that isn't actually applied to the live element.
